title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence · Behavior Case: Token Tree Model
summary: A selector works under one parser but fails under another because omitted elements, foster parenting, broken nesting, and script or noscript state produce different trees. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the token tree model increment in bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.
Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Token Tree Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-html-parser-models-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_html_parser_models_5(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"

# Evidence: scraper-token-tree-model-vex2if

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Token Tree Model, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the token tree model defect evidence boundary.

For repair the token tree model defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Token Tree Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 6713 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model, keep Token Tree Model connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Token Tree Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 6713 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Token Tree Model model · Theory Token Tree Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: The DOM is a lossless copy of the original HTML source.
